title: Figure 3 from: Jaume-Schinkel S, Müller B, Avila-Calero S, Kukowka S, Rduch V, Mengual X (2024) Preserving morphology while extracting DNA: a non-destructive field-to-museum protocol for slide-mounted specimens. Biodiversity Data Journal 12: e119448. https://doi.org/10.3897/BDJ.12.e119448
author: Jaume-Schinkel, Santiago
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 3 Graphic representation of the slide mounting technique. 1. dehydration process using different ethanol concentrations and clove oil (m = minutes); 2. preparation of the microscope slide and dissection of the specimens; 3. 24-hour lapse for drying the euparal and checking the correct positions of dissected body parts; 4. Placement cover glasses and drying process.
steps_raw: Figure 3 Graphic representation of the slide mounting technique. 1. dehydration process using different ethanol concentrations and clove oil (m = minutes); 2. preparation of the microscope slide and dissection of the specimens; 3. 24-hour lapse for drying the euparal and checking the correct positions of dissected body parts; 4. Placement cover glasses and drying process.
